question.text: Commercial Paper (CP) can be issued for a minimum denomination of:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per RBI regulations, Commercial Papers are issued for Rs. 5 lakh and multiples thereof, with maturities between 7 days and one year. They are discounted instruments issued in dematerialised form. **The Trap:** Certificate of Deposit (CD) also has the same minimum denomination of Rs. 5 lakh. Students may confuse CP with other instruments that have different minimums. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series IV covers CP and CD in the context of money market derivatives. Series VI (Depository Operations) covers the demat aspect. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Both CP and CD share the same minimum: Rs. 5 lakh. But CP is for corporates while CD is issued by banks (maturity up to 1 year) and AIFIs (1-3 years).
